title: 2025-26 Sacked Managers Tracker (Update)
text: The 2025-26 European football season has witnessed significant managerial changes across the continent's top leagues. Several high-profile managers have been relieved of their duties, with some securing positions elsewhere while others remain without clubs. Notable managerial movements include Nuno Espirito Santo's departure from Nottingham Forest, followed by his appointment at West Ham United. Jose Mourinho was sacked by Fenerbahce before joining SL Benfica. Igor Tudor left Juventus to become Tottenham's interim manager, and Habib Beye moved from Stade Rennais to Olympique de Marseille. Among managers sacked without subsequent employment are Ivan Juric from Atalanta, Ruben Amorim from Manchester United, and Sean Dyche from Nottingham Forest. Resignation announcements also marked the season. Enzo Maresca resigned from Chelsea in early 2026, with Liam Rosenior subsequently hired from RC Strasbourg. Rosenior was later sacked by Chelsea on April 22 following poor performances. Xabi Alonso and Real Madrid mutually parted ways on January 12 following their Copa del Rey defeat to Barcelona. The tracker examines managerial changes across ten European leagues: the top five including Bundesliga, LaLiga, Ligue 1, Premier League, and Serie A, plus six additional leagues including Eredivisie, Belgian Pro League, Primeira Liga, Super League 1, and Super Lig. Data was last updated May 11, 2026, excluding caretaker manager replacements.
